controls.label: Does your business have a Casinos process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Casinos process in place. You must establish a documented Casinos process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your casino designated service involve an amount less than $10,000 and relate to gaming chips, tokens, or similar gaming transactions (items 1, 2, 4, 6, 7, 8, or 9 in AML table 3)?
controls.detail-label: Describe the types of gaming transactions and their typical amounts
controls.remediation: Your casino Customer Identification Procedure must clarify which designated services qualify for the low-value exemption under rules 10.1.3(1) and 10.1.3(2). Identify the specific service types and amounts that may be exempt from Division 4 (customer identification) to ensure consistent application of CDD requirements.

controls.label: Does your casino designated service involve $10,000 or more but only for the giving or receiving of gaming chips or tokens (items 1, 2, 4, 6, or 9 in AML table 3)?
controls.detail-label: Describe the types of high-value transactions involving only gaming chips or tokens
controls.remediation: Your casino Customer Identification Procedure must clarify which high-value designated services qualify for the tokens-only exemption under rules 10.1.4(1) and 10.1.4(2). Confirm that transactions involve only gaming chips or tokens (not cash, credit, or other services) to determine whether the CDD exemption applies.

controls.label: Does your casino apply any CDD exemptions (low-value or tokens-only) without overriding them based on your risk assessment?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for determining when exemptions are overridden by EDD or CIP requirements
controls.remediation: Your casino Customer Identification Procedure fails to implement the mandatory override rule in 10.1.5. You must obtain and verify KYC information whenever your enhanced customer due diligence program or customer identification program determines that KYC information is necessary, regardless of any exemption. Update your procedure to ensure exemptions do not prevent collection of required customer information.

controls.label: For customer identification verification, does your casino complete the specified action within 14 days after the circumstance comes into existence, or before commencing another designated service to that customer?
controls.detail-label: Describe your timeline for completing customer verification for casino customers
controls.remediation: Your casino Verification of Identity procedure does not comply with the modified timeframe in rule 10.1.6(1). You must complete identity verification within 14 days starting after the circumstance specified in 6.1.2 comes into existence, or before commencing another designated service to the customer, whichever is earlier. Update your procedure to reflect the 14-day deadline for casino customers.

controls.label: For suspicious matter reporting, does your casino complete the specified action within 14 days after the reporting obligation arises, or before commencing another designated service to that customer?
controls.detail-label: Describe your timeline for reporting suspicious matters for casino customers
controls.remediation: Your casino AML reporting procedure does not comply with the modified timeframe in rule 10.1.6(2). You must complete suspicious matter reporting within 14 days starting after the reporting obligation arises, or before commencing another designated service to the customer, whichever is earlier. Update your procedure to reflect the 14-day deadline for casino customers.

controls.label: For ongoing customer due diligence, does your casino complete the specified action within 14 days after the reporting obligation arises, or before commencing another designated service to that customer?
controls.detail-label: Describe your timeline for completing ongoing due diligence for casino customers
controls.remediation: Your casino ongoing due diligence procedure does not comply with the modified timeframe in rule 10.1.6(3). You must complete the specified action within 14 days starting after the reporting obligation arises, or before commencing another designated service to the customer, whichever is earlier. Update your procedure to reflect the 14-day deadline for casino customers.

controls.label: Does your casino recognize that paragraphs 6.2.3 and 6.3.3 of Chapter 6 do not apply to casino designated services?
controls.remediation: Your casino procedure fails to comply with rule 10.1.6(4), which excludes paragraphs 6.2.3 and 6.3.3 from applying to casino designated services. Ensure your procedure acknowledges that Chapter 6 verification rules are modified and that 6.2.3 and 6.3.3 do not apply.

controls.label: Does your casino apply the record-keeping exemptions for gaming transactions (items 1, 2, 6 in AML table 3, or item 4 when only gaming chips/tokens are provided)?
controls.detail-label: Describe which gaming transactions are exempt from sections 106 and 107 record-keeping requirements
controls.remediation: Your casino Record Keeping procedure must clarify which designated services qualify for the record-keeping exemption under rules 10.1.7(1) and 10.1.7(2). Identify specific transaction types (items 1, 2, 6 of AML table 3, or item 4 with tokens only) that are exempt from sections 106 and 107 to ensure appropriate record retention.

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: Low-Value Transaction Exemption
groups.description: Designated services under $10,000 involving gaming chips, tokens, or similar transactions may be exempt from customer identification requirements under Division 4 of Part 2.

groups.title: Tokens-Only Transaction Exemption
groups.description: Designated services of $10,000 or more that involve only the giving or receiving of gaming chips or tokens may be exempt from customer identification requirements under Division 4 of Part 2.

groups.title: CDD Exemption Override Mechanism
groups.description: Any exemption to customer identification requirements must not prevent your casino from obtaining and verifying KYC information if your enhanced due diligence program or customer identification program determines that information is necessary.

groups.title: Customer Identification Verification Timing
groups.description: Casino customers require modified timelines for identity verification compared to other designated services.

groups.title: Suspicious Matter Reporting and Ongoing Due Diligence Timing
groups.description: Casino designated services are subject to modified timelines for suspicious matter reporting and ongoing due diligence as set out in Chapter 6.

groups.title: Record-Keeping Exemptions for Gaming Transactions
groups.description: Certain gaming transactions at casinos are exempt from the standard record-keeping requirements under sections 106 and 107 of the AML/CTF Act.
